title: Yangzijiang Maritime places major VLCC bet with eight-ship order
content: Yangzijiang Maritime Development is doubling down on crude tankers, lining up a major series of VLCC newbuilds as it looks to scale up in the large tanker segment. The Singapore-listed company said it is investing in eight 319,000 dwt units at a Chinese yard, with deliveries spread across 2028, 2029 and 2030. The VLCCs will …

title: COSCO adds panamax tankers to orderbook
content: COSCO Shipping Energy Transportation (CSET) is adding to its already sizeable orderbook, booking more tanker tonnage. The listed oil and gas shipping business of COSCO Shipping Group said its subsidiary, Hainan Cosco Shipping Energy, has ordered two 74,900 dwt panamax crude tankers at Cosco Shipping Offshore Qidong. The newbuilds are priced at about $76.5m each …

title: Container freight rally ends as global shipping rates fall - InteriorDaily
content: Container freight rally ends as global shipping rates fall  InteriorDaily

title: Himalaya Shipping scores fresh premium index-linked charter
content: Oslo-listed Himalaya Shipping has lined up fresh employment for its newcastlemax, keeping the LNG dual-fuel bulker trading at a premium to the wider capesize market. The Tor Olav Troim-backed owner said the 2024-built 210,000 dwt Mount Emai has secured a charter for 12 to 14 months, starting in the first half of May immediately after …

title: Performance Shipping secures Repsol charters for suezmax newbuilds
content: Performance Shipping has locked in long-term employment for its two suezmax newbuildings well ahead of delivery. The Nasdaq-listed Greek owner said it has signed charter deals with Repsol Trading for the two 158,000 dwt vessels currently under construction in China at Shanghai Waigaoqiao Shipbuilding. The first ship has been fixed for seven years at $35,000 …
